title: How We Helped Craftwood Scale Their Online Presence
resultHeadline: 150% increase in organic traffic in 90 days

blocks.title: The Challenge
blocks.body: Craftwood Flooring Co. had a strong local reputation but almost no online presence. Their existing website was outdated, slow, and wasn't appearing in any local search results. Leads were coming almost entirely from word of mouth — reliable, but not scalable. They needed a digital foundation that could match the quality of their craftsmanship.

blocks.title: Our Approach
blocks.body: We started with a full audit of their existing site and competitive landscape. From there, we designed a clean, conversion-focused website built on a modern CMS with local SEO baked in from day one. Every page was structured to drive visitors toward a consultation booking — with fast load times, clear calls to action, and social proof front and centre.

blocks.title: What We Did
items: Full website redesign with conversion-focused layout
Local SEO optimisation — Google Business Profile, schema markup, and on-page foundations
CMS setup so the Craftwood team can update content without a developer
Speed and performance optimisation (99/100 Lighthouse score)
Attribution tracking to measure lead sources accurately

blocks.quote: Nudge The Web built us a website that not only looks amazing but actually works to convert. He understood our vision right away and translated it into a fast, modern site that's easy to update and designed to grow with our business.
blocks.author: Hayden McRae

blocks.title: The Outcome
blocks.body: Within 90 days of launch, organic traffic had grown by 150% and inbound leads had tripled. Craftwood now has a consistent pipeline of enquiries coming through their website — and a platform they can confidently grow on as their business expands.

title: Streamlining Revenue Operations for a Fast-Scaling B2B Firm

blocks.title: The Challenge
blocks.body: Apex Growth Partners were scaling fast but their internal operations weren't keeping up. Sales and marketing data lived in disconnected tools, pipeline reporting was manual and error-prone, and their HubSpot CRM was underutilised — acting as little more than a contact database rather than a revenue engine.

blocks.title: Disconnected Systems, Real Costs
blocks.body: Their team was spending over 10 hours per week manually compiling reports that should have been automatic. Deals were slipping through cracks because there was no clear view of the pipeline, and attribution was essentially guesswork. They needed a single source of truth across sales, marketing, and customer success.

blocks.title: What We Delivered
items: Full HubSpot CRM audit and restructure — clean, consistent data across all contacts and deals
Automated pipeline stage progression based on deal activity and engagement signals
Custom reporting dashboards for sales leadership — live, not manual
Marketing attribution setup connecting ad spend to closed revenue
Zapier and native HubSpot automation workflows to eliminate repetitive manual tasks
Team onboarding and documentation so the system is maintainable long-term

blocks.quote: We finally have a CRM that actually works for us. The reporting alone has saved us hours every week, and we now have full confidence in our pipeline numbers going into every board meeting.
blocks.author: Sarah Okafor
blocks.role: Head of Revenue, Apex Growth Partners

title: Building a Data-Driven Marketing Engine from the Ground Up

blocks.title: The Challenge
blocks.body: Northsea Digital were spending a significant budget across Google and Meta ads but had no reliable way to measure what was actually working. Tracking was broken — events were firing incorrectly, conversion data was inconsistent, and their agency had no accurate signal to optimise against. They were flying blind.

blocks.title: Our Approach
blocks.body: We began with a full tracking audit — identifying every broken event, duplicate trigger, and misconfigured conversion. From there we rebuilt their GA4 and Meta Pixel setup from scratch using server-side tagging via Google Tag Manager. Once the data foundation was solid, we restructured their paid campaigns using the clean conversion signals.

blocks.title: What We Built
items: Full GA4 implementation — events, conversions, audiences, and custom dimensions
Server-side GTM setup to improve data accuracy and reduce browser-side data loss
Meta Conversions API integration for first-party signal matching
Google Ads conversion tracking rebuilt from scratch with verified attribution
Custom Looker Studio dashboard for live campaign performance reporting
Automated anomaly alerts for tracking breaks or spend spikes

blocks.quote: We had no idea how broken our tracking was until Nudge The Web audited it. Within two weeks we had clean data, and within a month our campaign performance had transformed. We're now making decisions based on real numbers.
blocks.author: Tom Reilly

blocks.title: The Outcome
blocks.body: With accurate tracking in place for the first time, Northsea's media team could finally optimise with confidence. ROAS improved by 2.8× within 60 days — not because we changed the creative, but because the algorithm now had accurate signals to learn from. Eliminating wasted spend on mis-attributed conversions freed up budget that was reinvested into high-performing campaigns.
